description:
The closure-to-fixpoint loop (lines 572-627) re-walks every registered spec each iteration to find new pending keys:

  let snapshot: Vec<_> = specs.keys().cloned().collect();
  for (fid, key) in &snapshot { walk_spec_for_discovery(...) }

A newly-registered spec can generate new pending keys. A stable spec (already walked, no change to specs or effective_returns since last walk) cannot — it would generate the same keys it already generated, which are already in specs and would be filtered by push_key.

Replace: maintain a worklist Vec<(FnId, Vec<Descr>)> of specs registered this round (from drain_and_register's return value, per fz-afs.7/W0). Each iteration: walk only the worklist specs, collect new registrations, repeat. Terminate when the worklist is empty.

The full-snapshot clone and the O(all_specs) re-walk per iteration are removed. Work is proportional to new registrations, not total spec count.

Add a test: a module with many independent fns; verify type_module produces the same ModuleTypes as before but with fewer walk_spec_for_discovery calls (instrument via a counter or check spec-count stability).

Requires fz-afs.7 (W0) for drain_and_register's return value.
title: replace full-spec re-walk with worklist in closure-to-fixpoint
